As foreigners, we sometimes felt overlooked, which was disappointing. We sincerely hope the management can improve consistency in both food and service, because this place has great potential.

review_text:
This was one of the worst experiences I have encountered.

My mother is 65 years old and wanted to bring her friend, who is 66. We were informed that your policy does not allow members above the age of 65. I have never seen such a rule anywhere in the world.

Life expectancy is increasing, people are living longer, and staying physically active is a cornerstone of healthy aging. Exercise is recommended for all ages, and health risks exist at any age — not just after 65.

Denying access based solely on age is discriminatory, outdated, and completely contradicts modern health and wellness principles. What is a 65-year-old expected to do if they want to maintain a healthy, active lifestyle?

This policy reflects a lack of understanding of today’s medical and fitness standards, and it sends a very negative message about inclusivity and respect for older age

Overpriced and inconsistent pricing – Better options nearby!
"To be honest, while the food was clean, the taste was very average and didn't live up to the hype. The seating is very basic (street-side), which doesn't justify the extravagant and inconsistent prices.
The biggest issue is the lack of integrity in billing; they seem to charge 'by the look' or nationality—one price for foreigners, another for Gulf tourists, and even the price for Egyptians is way above market standards. Charging 500 EGP for a simple breakfast of beans (Foul), eggs, and potatoes is pure exploitation.
If you want an authentic, delicious, and fairly priced Egyptian breakfast, I highly recommend going to Al Gahsh (?????) or Bashandi (?????). They offer much better quality, better taste, and most importantly, honest pricing for everyone. Don't waste your money here."
